name: Indian Oil Marketing (IOC/BPCL/HPCL)
summary: Day 80 (Monday). **BRENT $111.47 +2.0% MONDAY (StanChart $130-145 path activates — 2-session sustain rule met) — OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE NOW DEEPLY OUT-OF-RANGE; STRUCTURAL DISRUPTION FRAME REASSERTING.** Hardeep Puri's ₹1,000 cr/day PSU OMC under-recovery quantification (May 13) is operative; total under-recovery on track ₹1.98 lakh cr. **A6 USD/INR ₹96.29 record low (Day 6 of 5-7 trigger fires)** compounds the import-bill pressure on PSU OMCs: every $1 above ~$83 baseline adds ~$1.6-2B/yr to India's import bill; at $111 + ₹96.29, incremental CAD drain ~$45-55B annualised vs pre-crisis. **B2 Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04% Monday** confirms broader risk-off; PSU OMC equity overhang persists. Modi-MBZ MoUs from May 15 (IndianOil-ADNOC LPG framework + ISPRL-ADNOC 30 mn bbl + Indian crude at Fujairah outside Hormuz) provide structural Hormuz-bypass hedge but DON'T resolve current pricing/under-recovery dynamics. Nationwide fuel price hike reported May 15 — politically-explosive pass-through. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + Hormuz operational reopening — neither imminent. **PRIOR (May 16): BRENT $109.26 +3.3% 1D / WTI $105.42 +4.2% — OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE NOW FULLY OUT-OF-RANGE; structural-disruption frame reasserting through WTI +11% weekly. Hardeep Puri ₹1,000 cr/day PSU OMC under-recovery (₹1.98 lakh cr on track) is the operative fiscal-pressure framing.** **A4 MODI-MBZ MoUs SIGNED MAY 15 with long-term LPG SALE-AND-PURCHASE FRAMEWORK BETWEEN INDIANOIL AND ADNOC** (CONFIRMED — Business Standard, The Tribune, The Wire) = structural India-LPG architecture cemented; provides operational hedge against Hormuz disruption for IOC specifically. **B7 nationwide fuel price hike (reported May 15)** — politically-explosive pass-through that yesterday's brief flagged as trigger condition is firing. PSU OMC equity overhang persists; Reliance refining-margin tailwind diverges from OMC marketing-margin compression. **The MOU framework as structural relief catalyst remains operative**: would require Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + Hormuz operational reopening. Neither imminent. PRIOR (May 7): **OMC RELIEF ENVELOPE NOW IN-RANGE: BRENT AT $101.95 MAY 7 MORNING (-7% MAY 6 ON MOU SCOOP); WORLD BANK $86 AVERAGE FOR 2026 + GOLDMAN Q2 $90 = INSTITUTIONAL FORECAST BAND BELOW INDIAN OMC LOSS-THRESHOLD.** Brent at $101 compresses CAD pressure significantly vs $115 — but OMCs remain capped because (1) Hormuz still operationally closed (5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm; Trump pause preserved blockade explicitly); (2) $101 still above India's structural-loss-threshold (~$85-90 ceiling without pump-price pass-through); (3) Iran's first unconditional Hormuz reopening offer of cycle is REPORTED (PBS/AP/Al Jazeera/IRNA) but NOT yet operationally translated. The OMC forced-choice 30-day window (started ~Apr 13) is at Day ~24, expiring ~May 27. RBI special-credit-facility for OMC USD-demand REMAINS ACTIVE. CHABAHAR remains closed (US waiver expired April 26). **The MOU framework is the structural relief catalyst:** if signed within 14 days (by May 21) AND Hormuz operationally reopens (transit volume above 30/day for 3 consecutive days), OMC compressed-spread trade unwinds — Brent toward $86-90 (Goldman/World Bank) materially improves IOC/BPCL/HPCL margin profile. Watch Araghchi-India May 7-8 for any signal on India-Iran rupee-settlement oil track restart (would re-open the Chabahar escape hatch).

active_triggers.condition: Government instructs OMCs to freeze fuel prices despite $110+ oil
OMC fuel-price pass-through announced OR Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar restart within 30 days (by 2026-05-27)

recent_signals.headline: **OMC PASS-THROUGH ACTIVATED — TWO ~90 PAISE PETROL/DIESEL HIKES IN A WEEK; APRIL 27 FUEL-PASS-THROUGH TRIGGER FIRES INSIDE 30-DAY WINDOW.** The Modi government chose to break the political seal — pre-2024-election it deferred price hikes for 5 quarters; choosing TWO hikes in a week now signals fiscal patience is exhausted. **Mechanism**: India imports ~85% of crude; sustained Brent $109-111 + USD/INR ₹96 + the May 1 weekly-reserves drawdown of $7.79B forced OMCs to either continue absorbing the import-bill shock (IOC/BPCL/HPCL Q4 margin destruction, 3 weeks of stock-price punishment of OMCs has been pricing this) or pass it through to consumers. **The government chose passthrough — margin-protective for OMCs but politically explosive.** Pre-election the deferral was political; the pass-through now is policy. **Follow-on**: 90 paise increase = ~0.85% inflation contribution if sustained; food/transport inflation will follow with 2-3 week lag.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A4; Markets dossier C6
recent_signals.verification: India fuel hike ~90 paise = CONFIRMED ACTION (WION + Goodreturns multi-source); second hike in a week = CONFIRMED; OMC pass-through choice = CONFIRMED operational/political signal.

historical_summaries.summary: 11 signals. Government slashed excise duty: petrol from ₹13 to ₹3/liter, diesel duty eliminated entirely (March 27, effective) | Oil at $109-112 keeps marketing margins deeply NEGATIVE for IOC, BPCL, HPCL | Indian crude basket at $120
